topic: Flagship partial-identification upgrade for factorial difference-in-differences: characterize the sharp identified set for causal moderation in a finite 2x2 factorial DiD design when the standard FDID estimand identifies only effect modification, allowing a bounded imbalance budget between moderator strata in untreated trends. Recover the recent factorial-DID point-identification result at zero imbalance, give an exact finite-cell LP/dual interval for moderation under nonzero imbalance, and exhibit a concrete witness where effect modification and causal moderation have opposite signs.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp finite-cell identified set for FDID average causal moderation when factorial parallel trends is relaxed to a bounded moderator-stratum untreated-trend imbalance budget.

source_refs.claim: FDID formalizes baseline factor G and exposure Z as a factorial DiD design; standard no-anticipation and parallel-trends assumptions identify effect modification, while causal moderation requires an additional factorial/generalized parallel-trends condition.
source_refs.author: Yiqing Xu, Anqi Zhao, Peng Ding
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: DiD sensitivity can be treated as partial identification under restrictions on deviations from parallel trends, with exact parallel trends as a zero-deviation special case.
source_refs.author: Ashesh Rambachan, Jonathan Roth

source_refs.claim: Bounded-variation assumptions illustrate the partial-ID pattern in which point-identifying invariance is recovered at zero tolerance and wider sets arise at positive tolerance.
source_refs.author: Charles F. Manski, John V. Pepper

source_refs.excerpt: the sharp box support of all treated-cohort and comparison-cohort anticipation distortions
the sharp identified set for ATT(g,t)
finite-dimensional support-function derivation and sharpness argument

open_problems.why_unsolved: Xu, Zhao, and Ding identify the exact gap: standard FDID assumptions recover effect modification but not causal moderation, and point identification of the latter requires the stronger factorial parallel-trends condition. Rambachan-Roth and Manski-Pepper show how zero-deviation identifying assumptions can be relaxed into partial-ID sensitivity regions, while the closest CausalSmith DID prior only solves bounded anticipation boxes for ATT. The missing object is the FDID-specific feasible set whose primitive nuisance is moderator-stratum untreated-trend imbalance, not an ATT contamination interval.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identification theorem giving lower and upper endpoints for the finite-cell causal-moderation projection, proving latent-completion sharpness, and showing collapse to the FDID point-identification formula at zero imbalance.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive the exact primal LP and matching dual interval for FDID causal moderation under nonzero imbalance, with shared moderator-stratum nuisance coordinates pooled before support evaluation.

source_refs.claim: Sharp causal bounds in finite discrete settings can be obtained by linear programming over latent potential-outcome or response-type completions.
source_refs.author: Alexander Balke, Judea Pearl
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Mean treatment-effect bounds with finite treatments and linear restrictions can be computed by a simple LP, adding or removing restrictions as linear constraints.
source_refs.author: Thomas Demuynck

source_refs.claim: DiD sensitivity can be treated as partial identification under restrictions on deviations from parallel trends, with exact parallel trends as a zero-deviation special case.
source_refs.author: Ashesh Rambachan, Jonathan Roth

source_refs.excerpt: summing signed coefficients before taking absolute values
same latent post-treatment trend discrepancy path perturbs every weighted aggregate simultaneously
Define the cohort-level unit-budget support in the target direction

open_problems.why_unsolved: Finite discrete partial-ID papers provide the LP technology, but they do not instantiate the FDID causal-moderation estimand or its moderator-stratum trend-imbalance constraints. Prior CausalSmith DID proposals repeatedly warn that separate coordinatewise intervals are not sharp when several weighted targets share one latent trend path. In FDID, the same imbalance coordinates enter multiple treatment-factor cells and contrasts, so sharpness requires one global LP or an equivalent dual support formula rather than subtracting independently bounded FDID contrasts.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-dimensional primal LP over cell means or latent potential-outcome completions, plus a dual endpoint formula whose coefficients pool all shared imbalance coordinates globally before taking supports.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the minimal nonzero imbalance budget under which FDID effect modification can have the opposite sign from average causal moderation, and exhibit a rational finite-cell witness attaining it.

source_refs.claim: FDID makes the distinction in panel/factorial DiD: standard assumptions recover effect modification, not causal moderation.
source_refs.author: Yiqing Xu, Anqi Zhao, Peng Ding
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Causal moderation means an intervention on the moderator would alter the treatment effect; treatment-effect heterogeneity alone does not establish that causal claim.
source_refs.author: Kirk Bansak
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series A

source_refs.claim: Causal interaction and effect modification are distinct notions, so subgroup heterogeneity need not equal a manipulable two-factor interaction.
source_refs.author: Tyler J. VanderWeele

source_refs.excerpt: this is an open anti-conservative stacked-weight region
explicit two-bin witness enumeration for the sign certificate

open_problems.why_unsolved: The moderation literature distinguishes subgroup effect heterogeneity from a counterfactual intervention on the moderator, and the FDID paper imports that distinction into panel factorial designs by showing that the ordinary estimand targets effect modification rather than causal moderation. Existing papers do not give a finite FDID table showing how large an untreated-trend imbalance must be for the two signs to diverge. CausalSmith reviewers have separately flagged explicit two-by-two witness tables as the right evidence for sign-frontier claims, but no prior banked proposal supplies the FDID moderation witness.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A threshold or sufficient-condition result for sign reversal, together with a small rational 2x2 FDID cell table satisfying the imbalance constraints where observed effect modification and feasible causal moderation are strictly opposite in sign.

open_problems.open_problem: Classify when an aggregate FDID-level sensitivity interval is equal to, conservative for, or anti-conservative relative to the sharp moderator-stratum cell-level moderation LP.

source_refs.excerpt: when aggregation changes the sensitivity object before inference begins
does not give a primitive quotient equality certificate
primitive active-face equality test comparing aggregate and cohort support frontiers

open_problems.why_unsolved: A tempting shortcut is to form the FDID contrast first and impose a scalar imbalance budget afterward. The panel_honest_did_phase and pid_tv_pretrend_atet bank entries warn that aggregation can change the sensitivity object before inference starts, but they did not solve the factorial-moderation quotient or give an equality certificate for aggregate versus cell-level support frontiers.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient equality certificate comparing the aggregate FDID support interval with the finite moderator-stratum LP projection, plus strict finite examples for conservative and anti-conservative failures.

literature_map:
1. Xu, Zhao, and Ding (2026, JASA) is the anchor: FDID treats a baseline factor G and exposure Z as a factorial DiD design.
2. Their central identification result says standard FDID assumptions identify effect modification, not causal moderation.
3. Causal moderation requires an additional factorial/generalized parallel-trends mean-independence condition.
4. Bansak (2021, JRSS A) and VanderWeele (2009, Epidemiology) supply the broader distinction between subgroup heterogeneity/effect modification and causal moderation/interaction.
5. Rambachan and Roth (2023, ReStud) provide the closest DiD sensitivity template: bounded deviations from parallel trends yield partial identification.
6. Manski and Pepper (2018, ReStat) provide a bounded-variation precedent where zero tolerance recovers point restrictions and positive tolerance yields ambiguity sets.
7. Balke and Pearl (1997, JASA) and Demuynck (2015, Economics Letters) justify finite LP sharp-bounds technology for discrete causal problems with linear restrictions.
8. Robust DiD bounds papers such as Ban and Kedagni are adjacent, but target treatment effects under selection/trend deviations rather than FDID causal moderation.
9. The web scan found no published result giving sharp FDID causal-moderation bounds under moderator-stratum untreated-trend imbalance.
10. The scan also found no published finite FDID witness where effect modification and causal moderation have opposite signs.
11. The live tension is therefore between a point-ID FDID moderation theorem at zero imbalance and the partial-ID/sensitivity literature for nonzero trend deviations.
12. Study-pipeline interval-inference gaps are adjacent for later inference work, but the current topic is the identification and sharpness substrate.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Active target folder only has state/pipeline metadata; no proposal text was available for pid_factorial_did_moderation_bounds_v1.
2. Closest accepted DID partial-ID prior is pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1: finite support-function sharpness and zero-bound recovery.
3. Its reusable geometry is pooled signed coefficients before absolute values, adapted here to moderator-stratum imbalance cells.
4. panel_honest_did_phase_v1 is the main warning source for aggregate-vs-cell sensitivity mismatch and quotient certificates.
5. pid_tv_pretrend_atet_v1 reinforces the shared latent trend-path geometry across multiple weighted targets.
6. pid_dose_response_iv_v1 contributes reviewer-tested guidance that sign claims need explicit two-bin or two-by-two witness tables.
7. q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends_p1_baseline_v2 is legacy overlap only; it names partial staggered-DID pretrends but not factorial moderation.
8. No prior proposal found a factorial-DID causal-moderation bound or an effect-modification/moderation opposite-sign witness.
